entries.title: Pippin’s Palantir Look Forces Gandalf’s Ride

context.label: Pippin’s Palantir Look Forces Gandalf’s Ride

content.fact: Pippin’s look into the palantir accelerates Gandalf’s ride to Minas Tirith.
content.injection: Pippin’s look into the palantir accelerates Gandalf’s ride to Minas Tirith. Before this anchor, do not assume its consequences have already happened.

entries.title: Pippin Enters Denethor’s Service

context.label: Pippin in Minas Tirith before Denethor falls

content.fact: Pippin enters the service of Denethor in Minas Tirith after feeling bound by Boromir’s death and Gondor’s need.
content.injection: In Minas Tirith scenes before Denethor falls, Pippin may serve in the Citadel and experience Gondor through the Steward’s court, fear, formality, and siege pressure.
constraints: Pippin remains a Hobbit and outsider even while wearing Gondorian livery.
antiLore: Do not keep Pippin traveling with Merry or Aragorn during the Minas Tirith siege.

entries.title: Aragorn Takes The Paths Of The Dead

context.label: Aragorn uses the Paths of the Dead before Pelennor arrival

content.fact: Aragorn takes the Paths of the Dead to summon the oath-bound Dead Men and turn the southern threat against Mordor’s allies.
content.injection: When the Paths of the Dead arc is active, Aragorn’s lineage has concrete supernatural and military consequence: only Isildur’s heir can command the oathbreakers to fulfill their ancient oath.
constraints: This route removes Aragorn, Legolas, and Gimli from the main Rohan ride until Aragorn’s later arrival by ship.
antiLore: Do not have Aragorn ride openly with Theoden to Minas Tirith if the Paths of the Dead route is active.

entries.title: The Paths Of The Dead Test Aragorn’s Legitimacy

context.label: The Paths Of The Dead Test Aragorn’s Legitimacy

content.fact: Aragorn’s passage works because of lineage, courage, command, and the Dead Men’s ancient oath.
content.injection: The Dead answer Aragorn’s rightful command through oath and fear, not because anyone can recruit ghosts.
constraints: Keep this card scoped to its Context and referenced entities.

context.label: Faramir Rides Under Impossible Orders

content.fact: Faramir’s sortie is shaped by Denethor’s grief, impossible expectation, and obedience despite danger.
content.injection: Faramir’s ride should feel dutiful and doomed, not rash glory-seeking.

entries.title: Minas Tirith Is Under Siege

context.label: Siege of Minas Tirith through Aragorn’s arrival

content.fact: Minas Tirith is besieged by Mordor, with fear, exhaustion, command strain, and collapsing hope shaping the city’s behavior.
content.injection: During the Siege of Minas Tirith, write Gondor as embattled and strained: walls, horns, smoke, fear, command breakdown, and desperate endurance should shape scenes.
constraints: The city is not merely a scenic capital during this window; it is under existential military pressure.
antiLore: Do not portray Minas Tirith as safe, normal court life during the active siege.

entries.title: Denethor’s Pyre Crisis Threatens Faramir

context.label: Denethor pyre through Houses of Healing

content.fact: Denethor, driven by despair, attempts to burn himself and the still-living Faramir on a funeral pyre during the siege.
content.injection: When Denethor’s pyre crisis is active, treat it as a culmination of despair and broken Stewardship, with Faramir’s life at immediate risk.
constraints: Pippin and Beregond are relevant to the attempt to save Faramir.
antiLore: Do not portray Denethor’s death as calm retirement or political resignation.

entries.title: Pelennor Turns The War Front

context.label: Battle of Pelennor and immediate aftermath

content.fact: The Battle of Pelennor Fields breaks the immediate siege of Minas Tirith through Rohan’s arrival, sacrifice, and Aragorn’s later arrival with southern forces.
content.injection: During Pelennor, keep the battle as a massive turning point: Rohan’s charge, Theoden’s fall, Eowyn and Merry’s deed, and Aragorn’s arrival all matter to the outcome.
constraints: The battle is not won by one single action; several timed reversals compound.
antiLore: Do not skip Rohan’s sacrifice or Aragorn’s arrival if portraying the book battle.

content.injection: Preserve the combined logic: Eowyn confronts him and Merry’s blade enables the fatal opening.

content.injection: The Captains of the West should understand the march as strategic self-sacrifice, not conventional invasion.

entries.title: Gollum’s Final Intervention Fulfills Pity And Chance

context.label: Gollum’s Final Intervention Fulfills Pity And Chance

content.fact: Gollum’s final struggle and fall fulfill long consequences of pity, obsession, chance, and corruption.
content.injection: The Ring’s destruction should come through Gollum’s intervention after Frodo fails, not a planned victory.
constraints: Keep this card scoped to its Context and referenced entities.
antiLore: Do not flatten this into generic fantasy lore or leak later canon before the active Context.

entries.title: Ring Destruction Causes Sauron’s Fall

context.label: After the Ring is destroyed

content.fact: The destruction of the One Ring destroys Sauron’s immediate power, collapses Mordor’s command, and ends the central War of the Ring threat.
content.injection: After the Ring is destroyed, do not continue portraying Sauron or Mordor as functioning with the same direct centralized power. The Enemy’s will has broken.
constraints: Enemy remnants and aftermath problems can remain, but Sauron’s ruling power is gone.
antiLore: Do not keep Sauron as an active commanding Dark Lord after the Ring’s destruction in book continuity.

entries.title: Cormallen Honors Are Relief Not Full Recovery

content.fact: Cormallen honors Frodo and Sam publicly and gives relief, but does not erase trauma or grief.
content.injection: Cormallen scenes can be joyful and ceremonial while preserving exhaustion, loss, and delayed aftermath.
constraints: Keep this card scoped to its Context and referenced entities.
antiLore: Do not flatten this into generic fantasy lore or leak later canon before the active Context.

content.injection: At Cormallen, Frodo and Sam move from secret burden to public honor among allies. Keep this hidden from uninformed characters until the relevant reveal or direct evidence.

entries.title: Scouring Of The Shire Is Book Aftermath

context.label: Scouring of the Shire through Grey Havens

content.fact: In the novel, the Hobbits return to find the Shire damaged and controlled by Sharkey’s ruffians, forcing them to lead its liberation.
content.injection: For book-continuity postwar scenes, include the Scouring of the Shire unless the user explicitly selects film continuity or an AU that omits it.
constraints: This is post-Ring destruction aftermath, not an early Shire condition.
antiLore: Do not import the film omission as default for this book-continuity deck.

context.label: The Scouring Shows The Hobbits Have Grown

content.fact: The returning Hobbits confront the Shire’s occupation with maturity, leadership, and courage gained from war.
content.injection: Scouring scenes should show the Hobbits as changed actors, not helpless villagers.

entries.title: The Grey Havens Close The Third Age For The Ring-bearers

context.label: The Grey Havens Close The Third Age For The Ring-bearers

content.fact: The Grey Havens departure removes Frodo, Bilbo, Gandalf, Elrond, Galadriel, and the Ring-bearers’ burden from ordinary Middle-earth.
content.injection: The Grey Havens should feel like sorrowful healing departure and age-ending transition, not simple happy ending.
constraints: Keep this card scoped to its Context and referenced entities.
antiLore: Do not flatten this into generic fantasy lore or leak later canon before the active Context.
